name: Manoa Honey & Mead
tagline: Hawaii's only meadery — taste local honey wines, tour the apiary, and learn about beekeeping on a working honey farm.
bestTime: Saturday for Bees to Mead tours at 10am or 2pm (reservations required). Weekday afternoons for unhurried tastings
talkStory: Don't let the Wahiawa industrial location fool you — this tiny meadery is doing something nobody else in Hawaii is. They make mead (honey wine) from their own hives and offer tastings of 6 varieties Friday through Sunday (last seating 5pm). The Bees to Mead tour ($10 deposit) takes you through the apiary to see honey harvesting up close before tasting. You can share tasting flights if 6 glasses is too much. The showroom sells local honey products and bottles to take home. It's small, it's not fancy, and that's exactly why it's great. 21+ for mead tastings.
